untranslatable_reason: This is an index arbitrage strategy requiring dual-leg execution (buy stocks, sell futures) with inputs that are not standard OHLCV price/volume indicators. The signal requires: (1) fair_value_premium calculation (not available as indicator), (2) actual_futures_premium (not available as indicator), (3) cross-asset comparison between stock index and futures contract, (4) precise cost accounting across multiple legs (stock commission, futures entry/exit costs). The backtest engine as specified can only work with single-asset price/technical indicators and cannot model multi-leg arbitrage execution or commission structures with per-share granularity.
translation_notes: Signal references AUTHOR_SILENT parameters (fair_value_premium, actual_futures_premium) that cannot be derived from the allowed indicator set. The core logic—'when futures premium exceeds fair value'—requires real-time premium calculation not expressible through standard technical indicators. Entry condition approximated as close > 1.75% (150 bps excess premium) and exit as close < 1.23% (entry cost basis), but this is not a valid translation of the intended arbitrage rule.
